parameters.text:
=Write a bilingual blog post for Accez.cloud about: {{ $json.topic }}.

CRITICAL FRONTMATTER RULES: ALL string values MUST be wrapped in double quotes, even if they contain colons, apostrophes, or quotes. Escape any internal double quotes with a backslash. Do not use unescaped colons inside string values.

Start the file with this exact MDX frontmatter (replace the bracketed parts):

---
title: "[English title here — no colons]"
titleAr: "[Arabic title here — no colons]"
excerpt: "[English excerpt here — no colons]"
excerptAr: "[Arabic excerpt here — no colons]"
date: "{{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}"
author: "Accez Team"
authorAr: "فريق أكسيز"
coverImage: "/images/blog/pm-default.jpg"
coverImageAlt: "[descriptive alt text without colons]"
category: "Industry Insights"
categoryAr: "رؤى الصناعة"
tags: ["tag1", "tag2", "tag3", "tag4", "tag5"]
tagsAr: ["وسم1", "وسم2", "وسم3", "وسم4", "وسم5"]
---

After the closing --- of the frontmatter, output the article body following this EXACT structure in this EXACT order. Do NOT add a title heading (#) at the top — start directly with prose.

=== STRUCTURE (follow exactly) ===

1. HOOK PARAGRAPH (no heading): 2-3 sentences in plain prose presenting a relatable problem or paradox that property managers face. Must hook immediately. Never start with generic phrases like "In the world of real estate..." or "In today's market...".

2. TRANSITION PARAGRAPH (no heading): One paragraph expanding on the problem.

3. ## [First main section heading] — a deep dive into what breaks and why. Write mostly prose paragraphs. Bullet points are allowed only when listing 4+ items. Bold maximum 2-3 critical phrases in this section. Include at least one real statistic with a source.

4. ## [Second main section heading, e.g. "What High Performers Do Differently"] — Use ### subheadings for each point. Each subsection must be 2-3 paragraphs of prose, NOT bullet lists. Bold maximum 2-3 critical phrases total in this section. Include at least one real statistic with a source.

5. ## [Saudi Arabia specific section heading] — Must reference at least TWO of these specifically: Vision 2030, Ejar compliance, WhatsApp adoption (77%+ rate in KSA), GASTAT statistics, Mada/SADAD payments, seasonal demand around Hajj/Umrah. Write in flowing prose, not lists. Include real statistics with sources.

6. ## How Accez.Cloud Helps You [topic-relevant verb] — MANDATORY section. This is the first time Accez.Cloud is introduced in the article. List 5-6 specific Accez.Cloud features as bullet points. Each bullet must directly solve a problem mentioned earlier in the article. End the section with one closing sentence about growth.

7. ## Key Takeaways — EXACTLY 6 to 8 bullet points summarizing the article's main points.

8. CTA line: One single line ending with [Visit portal.accez.cloud](https://portal.accez.cloud)

9. <!-- AR --> on its own line.

10. Complete Arabic translation following the EXACT same structure (steps 1-8 in Arabic). Same rules: no title heading, hook first, ## and ### only, prose-first, identical section order, mandatory "How Accez.Cloud Helps You" section before Key Takeaways, CTA at the end.

=== TONE & FORMATTING RULES ===

- Write like a knowledgeable industry consultant who understands Saudi property management deeply.
- Never introduce Accez.Cloud before section 6 in either language.
- Use ## and ### only — NEVER use #### or deeper.
- Prose-first. Bullets only when listing 4+ items. For unordered lists ALWAYS use `-` (hyphen) — NEVER `*` (asterisk). The site renderer only recognizes hyphen bullets; asterisk bullets render as raw text.
- Bold a maximum of 2-3 phrases per section, only the most important ones. Do not over-bold.
- Include at least one real statistic with a source per major section (sections 3, 4, 5).
- Opening must hook immediately. No generic openers.
- Length: 1500-1800 words English, 1500-1800 words Arabic.

=== OUTPUT RULES ===

Output raw markdown only. No code fences (no ``` anywhere). No explanation. Nothing before the frontmatter. Nothing after the Arabic CTA. The very first character of the output must be the opening --- of the frontmatter.

nodes.name: Mark Topic as Used in Sheet

assignments.name: message
assignments.value: ={{ 'Blog post deployed and sheet updated for topic: ' + $('Find First Pending Topic').first().json.topic }}

assignments.name: message
assignments.value: GitHub push failed. Google Sheet was NOT updated.

parameters.content:
## 1. Manual Trigger
Click 'Test workflow' to start the pipeline.

## 2. Read Google Sheets
Reads rows from **Topics** sheet. Code finds first row where column B = 'pending'.

## 3. AI Content Generation
Gemini (gemini-2.5-flash-lite) generates bilingual EN/AR blog post with MDX frontmatter.

## 4. Clean Output
Strips code fences and extracts the **article title** from frontmatter for Unsplash search.

## 5. Unsplash Image Fetch
**Search Unsplash** with article title → **Process Result** extracts the first image URL and updates `coverImage` in the MDX frontmatter.

Fallback: `/images/blog/pm-default.jpg` if no result.

**If Has Image** branches: TRUE → download + push image + article. FALSE → skip straight to article push.

## 6. Download & Push Image
Downloads the Unsplash image as binary and pushes to `public/images/blog/<slug>.jpg`.

## 7. Push Blog Post
Creates `content/blog/<slug>.md` on main. Error branches to failure node.

## 8. Conditional Sheet Update
Only runs if article push succeeds. Marks row as 'used' with today's date.

## 9. Output
Success: status + URL + topic.
Error: failure details, sheet unchanged.

assignments.name: facebookMessage
assignments.value:
={{ '📝 New Blog Post: ' + $('Strip Fences and Base64 Encode').first().json.articleTitle + '

' + (($('Process Unsplash Result').first().json.fileContent.match(/^excerpt:\s*"([^"]*)"/m) || [])[1] || '') + '

Read more: https://accez.cloud/blog/' + $('Process Unsplash Result').first().json.topicSlug + '

#AccezCloud #PropTech #RealEstate' }}

main.node: Mark Topic as Used in Sheet
